Why are you interested in this role?: What pulls me to this role is that I genuinely like being the person who keeps things running like clockwork while making people feel taken care of. At InGenius Prep I coordinate logistics and operations for 20+ programs across Marketing, Tech, and Sales teams—juggling a lot of moving pieces under tight timelines is where I'm at my best, and my supervisor has called me the most organized person he's ever worked with. A Recruiting Coordinator role is that instinct made into a job: owning the scheduling, keeping the ATS clean, and making sure every candidate has a smooth, warm experience from start to finish. I'd love to bring that to a team that's scaling as fast as Sierra.
Tell us about your experience with appli: I'll be honest—I haven't used Ashby specifically yet, but I have extensive hands-on experience as a Greenhouse administrator: posting roles, scheduling interviews, emailing candidates, and managing the talent pipeline and data. I'm the kind of person who triple-checks a calendar invite's timezone before sending, so keeping an ATS accurate and audit-ready is second nature to me. I pick up new platforms quickly and enjoy it, so I'm confident Ashby would come naturally within my first weeks.
How do you manage competing priorities /: I thrive under pressure as long as I stay organized—which I'm relentless about. My approach is to keep a clear daily plan (I'm a heavy Notion and Google Workspace user), triage by what's time-sensitive and who's waiting on me, and over-communicate status so nothing falls through the cracks. At InGenius I run multiple program workstreams at once across cross-functional teams, and at Vestas I coordinated interview schedules and communications with senior leadership while handling confidential information with discretion. Staying calm and reliable while holding many pieces together is genuinely where I do my best work.
Why Sierra?: I'm drawn to Sierra because you treat customer experience as something to be engineered to feel genuinely human, not just automated—and that mindset maps onto how I think about candidate experience. My graduate training in Applied Organizational Psychology is grounded in that same systems thinking: using data to find where a process breaks down and designing solutions that stick. I'd be excited to be the person who makes every candidate's first impression of Sierra a warm, well-run one.
